extra.message: SQL Injection vulnerability in Student.create(). User input from 'name' parameter is formatted directly into the SQL query using Python string formatting operator (%). The query uses "VALUES ('%(name)s')" with string interpolation instead of parameterized queries. An attacker can inject SQL commands via the student name field.

Stored XSS vulnerability. The review.review_text is rendered without escaping using the |e filter. Since autoescape is disabled in the application (autoescape=False in app.py), malicious JavaScript injected via the review form will execute in victims' browsers. The review system stores user input and displays it to all users.

Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability due to disabled autoescape. The Jinja2 templating engine is configured with autoescape=False, which prevents automatic HTML escaping of rendered variables. This allows attackers to inject malicious JavaScript through any user input field that is displayed without explicit escaping.

Insecure session cookie configuration. RedisStorage is initialized with httponly=False, making session cookies accessible to JavaScript. This allows session hijacking through XSS attacks. The httponly flag should be set to True to prevent client-side scripts from accessing the session cookie.

Use of cryptographically weak MD5 hash for password storage. The check_password method compares passwords using MD5 which is vulnerable to collision attacks and rainbow table attacks. User passwords should be hashed using bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 with proper salting.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: Application running in debug mode. The Application is initialized with debug=True which can expose sensitive information through error pages and stack traces. This should be set to False in production environments to prevent information disclosure.

Information disclosure in error handling. The 500 error template exposes the full error object dictionary (error.__dict__) to users. This can leak sensitive internal information including file paths, system configuration, and implementation details that could aid attackers.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message

extra.message: CSRF protection middleware is commented out. The csrf_middleware is disabled in the Application middlewares list. While CSRF tokens are present in forms, they are not being validated by the server. This allows attackers to perform Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks by tricking authenticated users into submitting malicious requests.

Reflected XSS vulnerability. The course.title and course.description variables are rendered without escaping in the courses list. Since autoescape is disabled globally, malicious JavaScript injected into course titles or descriptions will execute when users view the courses page.

Stored XSS vulnerability. The course.title and course.description are rendered without escaping using the |e filter. With autoescape disabled, any malicious JavaScript stored in the course title or description will execute when users view the course page.

Stored XSS vulnerability. User display names (auth_user.first_name, auth_user.middle_name, auth_user.last_name) are rendered without escaping in the navigation bar. With autoescape disabled, attackers could inject malicious JavaScript through user profile data that executes in all authenticated sessions.
